class.name: PiperTTS
class.brief_description: VITS neural TTS engine and phoneme inspection node for Godot.
class.description:
Integrates the piper-plus VITS neural text-to-speech engine into Godot as a node. It supports Japanese text via the builtin OpenJTalk path or an optional [code]openjtalk-native[/code] shared library, English text via the bundled CMU-based G2P path, capability-based multilingual routing, exact [member language_code] resolution with conflict validation, raw phoneme input, and inspection utilities that expose phoneme routing and timing metadata without requiring custom engine bindings. Set [member model_path] and any optional configuration members, then call [method initialize] before synthesis or inspection. [member model_path] can point to a concrete [code].onnx[/code] file, a directory that contains a single matching model, or a registered bundled model key/alias. If [member config_path] is empty, the node falls back to [code]<model>.json[/code] and then [code]config.json[/code] next to the resolved model. Use [method synthesize], [method synthesize_request], or [method synthesize_phoneme_string] for synchronous synthesis, [method synthesize_async] or [method synthesize_async_request] for worker-thread synthesis, and [method synthesize_streaming] or [method synthesize_streaming_request] for low-latency streamed playback. Use [method inspect_text], [method inspect_request], or [method inspect_phoneme_string] to inspect phoneme output without generating audio. The most recent synthesis and inspection metadata is cached in [method get_last_synthesis_result] and [method get_last_inspection_result], [method get_language_capabilities] exposes the loaded model contract, and [method get_last_error] exposes machine-readable validation and routing failures. For multilingual text input, [code]ja[/code] and [code]en[/code] can be auto-routed as preview languages, [code]es[/code] / [code]fr[/code] / [code]pt[/code] are experimental explicit-only adapters, and [code]zh[/code] remains phoneme-only because a text phonemizer is not implemented. The canonical support matrix is documented in [code]tests/fixtures/multilingual_capability_matrix.json[/code] and rendered for humans in [code]docs/generated/multilingual_capability_matrix.md[/code]. Set [member execution_provider] to use GPU acceleration on supported platforms (CoreML on macOS/iOS, DirectML on Windows, NNAPI on Android, CUDA when built with a CUDA-enabled ONNX Runtime package). Use [member gpu_device_id] to pick a non-default CUDA/DirectML device when the selected execution provider supports it. [codeblocks] [gdscript] # Basic usage example var tts = PiperTTS.new() tts.model_path = "ja_JP-test-medium" tts.dictionary_path = "res://addons/piper_plus/dictionaries/open_jtalk_dic_utf_8-1.11" tts.execution_provider = PiperTTS.EP_AUTO add_child(tts) tts.initialize()
# Async synthesis tts.synthesis_completed.connect(func(audio): $AudioStreamPlayer.stream = audio; $AudioStreamPlayer.play()) tts.synthesize_async("こんにちは、世界！")
# Phoneme inspection var inspected = tts.inspect_text("hello from godot") print(inspected.get("phoneme_sentences", [])) [/gdscript] [/codeblocks]

method.name: initialize
method.description: Loads the resolved ONNX model, applies [member config_path] fallback lookup when needed, configures the OpenJTalk dictionary, applies [member openjtalk_library_path] and [member custom_dictionary_path] if set, and initializes the TTS engine. [member model_path] must be set before calling this method. Returns [constant OK] on success, or an error code on failure. The [signal initialized] signal is also emitted when initialization completes.

method.name: synthesize
method.description: Synthesizes speech from the given text synchronously and returns the resulting audio as an [AudioStreamWAV] (22050 Hz, 16-bit PCM). This method blocks the calling thread until synthesis is complete. Returns [code]null[/code] if the engine has not been initialized or synthesis fails. On success, [method get_last_synthesis_result] is updated with timing and performance metadata for the generated audio.

method.name: synthesize_request
method.description: Flexible synchronous synthesis entry point. The request must contain exactly one input source: [code]text[/code], [code]phoneme_string[/code], or [code]phonemes[/code]. [code]phonemes[/code] may be a [PackedStringArray], [Array], or [String], and is normalized into a single raw phoneme string before synthesis. Optional per-request overrides include [code]speaker_id[/code], [code]language_id[/code], [code]language_code[/code], [code]speech_rate[/code], [code]noise_scale[/code], [code]noise_w[/code], [code]sentence_silence_seconds[/code] (or the alias [code]sentence_silence[/code]), and [code]phoneme_silence_seconds[/code] (or the alias [code]phoneme_silence[/code]). Returns [code]null[/code] on validation or synthesis failure. On success, [method get_last_synthesis_result] is updated. The result dictionary contains [code]input_mode[/code], [code]sample_rate[/code], [code]infer_seconds[/code], [code]audio_seconds[/code], [code]real_time_factor[/code], [code]has_timing_info[/code], [code]phoneme_timings[/code], [code]sentence_silence_seconds[/code], [code]phoneme_silence_seconds[/code], [code]resolved_language_id[/code], [code]resolved_language_code[/code], [code]selection_mode[/code], and [code]resolved_segments[/code].

method.name: synthesize_phoneme_string
method.description: Synthesizes speech synchronously from a raw phoneme string instead of text. This is a convenience wrapper around [method synthesize_request] with [code]phoneme_string[/code] set. Returns [code]null[/code] if the node is not initialized or the phoneme string cannot be synthesized. On success, [method get_last_synthesis_result] is updated and reports [code]input_mode = "phoneme_string"[/code].

method.name: synthesize_async
method.description: Synthesizes speech from the given text asynchronously on a worker thread. This method does not block the main thread. On success, the [signal synthesis_completed] signal is emitted with the resulting [AudioStreamWAV]. On failure, the [signal synthesis_failed] signal is emitted with an error message. Returns [constant OK] if the synthesis task was started, [constant ERR_UNCONFIGURED] if the node is not initialized, [constant ERR_INVALID_PARAMETER] if the text is empty or the language selection is invalid, or [constant ERR_BUSY] if synthesis is already in progress. On success, [method get_last_synthesis_result] is updated when the deferred completion handler runs.

method.name: synthesize_async_request
method.description: Asynchronous worker-thread variant of [method synthesize_request]. The request schema matches [method synthesize_request], including [code]text[/code], [code]phoneme_string[/code], or [code]phonemes[/code] plus the same per-request overrides. On success, the [signal synthesis_completed] signal is emitted with the resulting [AudioStreamWAV]. On failure, the [signal synthesis_failed] signal is emitted with an error message. [method get_last_synthesis_result] is updated when the deferred completion handler runs.

method.name: stop
method.description: Cancels the currently running asynchronous synthesis started by [method synthesize_async] or [method synthesize_async_request], or streaming synthesis started by [method synthesize_streaming] or [method synthesize_streaming_request]. Does nothing if no synthesis is in progress.

method.name: is_ready
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the TTS engine has been successfully initialized and is ready to synthesize speech.

method.name: inspect_text
method.description: Inspects the given text without generating audio and returns a dictionary describing the resolved phoneme sentences. The returned dictionary includes [code]phoneme_sentences[/code], [code]phoneme_id_sentences[/code], [code]missing_phonemes[/code], [code]resolved_language_id[/code], [code]resolved_language_code[/code], [code]selection_mode[/code], [code]resolved_segments[/code], [code]input_mode[/code], [code]sentence_silence_seconds[/code], and [code]phoneme_silence_seconds[/code]. Returns an empty dictionary if the node is not initialized, synthesis is already running, validation fails, or inspection throws an error. On success, [method get_last_inspection_result] is updated.

method.name: inspect_request
method.description: Flexible inspection entry point that accepts the same request dictionary schema as [method synthesize_request], but performs phonemization and routing inspection only. Raw phoneme input remains grouped as a single sentence in [code]phoneme_sentences[/code] and [code]phoneme_id_sentences[/code]. On success the dictionary also reports [code]selection_mode[/code] and [code]resolved_segments[/code] so request routing can be compared directly with [method synthesize_request]. Returns an empty dictionary on validation failure or if the node is busy or uninitialized. On success, [method get_last_inspection_result] is updated. When phoneme input is supplied, the dictionary reports [code]input_mode = "phoneme_string"[/code].

method.name: inspect_phoneme_string
method.description: Inspects a raw phoneme string without generating audio. This is a convenience wrapper around [method inspect_request] with [code]phoneme_string[/code] set. Returns an empty dictionary on failure. On success, [method get_last_inspection_result] is updated and reports [code]input_mode = "phoneme_string"[/code].

method.name: get_last_synthesis_result
method.description: Returns the most recent synthesis metadata dictionary produced by [method synthesize], [method synthesize_request], [method synthesize_phoneme_string], [method synthesize_async], [method synthesize_async_request], [method synthesize_streaming], or [method synthesize_streaming_request]. The dictionary is empty until a synthesis succeeds. On success it contains [code]input_mode[/code], [code]sample_rate[/code], [code]infer_seconds[/code], [code]audio_seconds[/code], [code]real_time_factor[/code], [code]has_timing_info[/code], [code]phoneme_timings[/code], [code]sentence_silence_seconds[/code], [code]phoneme_silence_seconds[/code], [code]resolved_language_id[/code], [code]resolved_language_code[/code], [code]selection_mode[/code], and [code]resolved_segments[/code]. Each item in [code]phoneme_timings[/code] is a dictionary with [code]phoneme[/code], [code]start_time[/code], [code]end_time[/code], [code]start_frame[/code], and [code]end_frame[/code]. Each item in [code]resolved_segments[/code] includes [code]text[/code], [code]language_code[/code], [code]language_id[/code], [code]selection_mode[/code], and [code]is_phoneme_input[/code].

method.name: get_last_inspection_result
method.description: Returns the most recent inspection metadata dictionary produced by [method inspect_text], [method inspect_request], or [method inspect_phoneme_string]. The dictionary is empty until an inspection succeeds. On success it contains [code]phoneme_sentences[/code], [code]phoneme_id_sentences[/code], [code]missing_phonemes[/code], [code]resolved_language_id[/code], [code]resolved_language_code[/code], [code]selection_mode[/code], [code]resolved_segments[/code], [code]input_mode[/code], [code]sentence_silence_seconds[/code], and [code]phoneme_silence_seconds[/code].

method.name: get_language_capabilities
method.description: Returns a capability dictionary for the loaded model. The dictionary includes [code]has_language_id_map[/code], [code]available_language_codes[/code], [code]available_language_ids[/code], [code]default_language_code[/code], [code]configured_language_code[/code], [code]configured_language_id[/code], [code]auto_route_language_codes[/code], [code]explicit_only_language_codes[/code], [code]text_supported_language_codes[/code], [code]phoneme_only_language_codes[/code], [code]preview_language_codes[/code], [code]experimental_language_codes[/code], [code]supports_text_input[/code], and [code]languages[/code]. Each item in [code]languages[/code] includes [code]language_code[/code], [code]language_id[/code], [code]support_tier[/code], [code]frontend_backend[/code], [code]routing_mode[/code], [code]text_supported[/code], [code]auto_supported[/code], and [code]phoneme_only[/code].

method.name: get_last_error
method.description: Returns the most recent machine-readable error record. The dictionary is empty until a validation or synthesis failure occurs. On failure it contains [code]code[/code], [code]message[/code], [code]stage[/code], and when available [code]requested_language_code[/code], [code]requested_language_id[/code], [code]resolved_language_code[/code], [code]resolved_language_id[/code], and [code]selection_mode[/code].

method.name: get_runtime_state
method.description: Returns the current runtime state as one of [code]"uninitialized"[/code], [code]"initializing"[/code], [code]"ready"[/code], [code]"busy"[/code], or [code]"stopping"[/code].

method.name: is_processing
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if an asynchronous synthesis started by [method synthesize_async] or [method synthesize_async_request], or a streaming synthesis started by [method synthesize_streaming] or [method synthesize_streaming_request] is currently in progress.

method.name: synthesize_streaming
method.description: Synthesizes speech from the given text using streaming mode. Audio chunks are pushed to the provided [param playback] as each sentence is synthesized, significantly reducing time-to-first-audio for long texts. The [param playback] must be obtained from an [AudioStreamPlayer] whose stream is set to an [AudioStreamGenerator] with [code]mix_rate = 22050[/code] and [code]buffer_length = 0.5[/code] (or larger). Call [method AudioStreamPlayer.play] before calling this method. When all audio has been pushed to the playback, the [signal streaming_ended] signal is emitted. On failure, the [signal synthesis_failed] signal is emitted. Returns [constant OK] if streaming synthesis was started, or [constant ERR_BUSY] if a synthesis is already in progress.

method.name: synthesize_streaming_request
method.description: Streaming variant of [method synthesize_request]. The request schema matches [method synthesize_request], including raw phoneme input. The [param playback] requirements are the same as [method synthesize_streaming]. When all audio has been pushed to the playback, the [signal streaming_ended] signal is emitted and [method get_last_synthesis_result] contains the same routing metadata shape as [method synthesize_request], including [code]selection_mode[/code] and [code]resolved_segments[/code]. On failure, the [signal synthesis_failed] signal is emitted.

members.member: Model selector used during [method initialize]. This may be a direct [code].onnx[/code] path, a directory that contains a single matching model, or a registered bundled model key/alias such as [code]ja_JP-test-medium[/code]. Supports Godot resource paths (for example [code]res://[/code] and [code]user://[/code]).

Optional path to the model configuration file ([code].json[/code]). If empty, [method initialize] searches for [code]<resolved_model>.json[/code] first and [code]config.json[/code] in the model directory second.

Path to the OpenJTalk dictionary directory (naist-jdic). Required for Japanese TTS. Supports Godot resource paths such as [code]res://[/code] and [code]user://[/code] (e.g., [code]res://addons/piper_plus/dictionaries/naist-jdic[/code]). When set, this path is applied as the OpenJTalk dictionary path during [method initialize].

Optional path to an [code]openjtalk-native[/code] shared library ([code].dll[/code], [code].so[/code], or [code].dylib[/code]). If this path is empty, the environment/default search path is used. If loading fails, the engine falls back to the builtin OpenJTalk backend.
Optional path to a custom dictionary JSON file that is loaded during [method initialize] and applied before phonemization. Supports Godot resource paths.
Speaker ID for multi-speaker models. Valid range is 0 to 999. Ignored for single-speaker models.

Optional explicit language ID override for multilingual models. A negative value means automatic selection. If both [member language_id] and [member language_code] are set, they must resolve to the same canonical language or initialization / request validation fails.

Optional language code override for multilingual models. Codes are normalized by trimming whitespace, lowercasing, and treating underscores and hyphens as equivalent during lookup, so values such as [code]EN_us[/code] and [code]FR_fr[/code] are accepted when the model exposes matching canonical codes like [code]en[/code] and [code]fr[/code]. Exact canonical matches are preferred first, then base-code fallbacks are used if needed. [code]ja[/code] and [code]en[/code] are preview auto-route languages when the model supports them, [code]es[/code] / [code]fr[/code] / [code]pt[/code] are experimental explicit-only adapters, and [code]zh[/code] is phoneme-only because a Chinese text phonemizer is not implemented. The canonical support matrix is recorded in [code]tests/fixtures/multilingual_capability_matrix.json[/code] and rendered in [code]docs/generated/multilingual_capability_matrix.md[/code].

Speech rate multiplier corresponding to the VITS [code]lengthScale[/code] parameter. A value of [code]1.0[/code] is the default speed. Smaller values produce faster speech, and larger values produce slower speech.
Controls the randomness of the generated speech. A value of [code]0.0[/code] produces the most stable output. Higher values introduce more variation in pitch and tone.
Controls the randomness of phoneme durations. A value of [code]0.0[/code] produces the most uniform timing. Higher values introduce more variation in the duration of individual phonemes.
Default silence inserted between synthesized sentences, in seconds. Per-request overrides can be supplied through [method synthesize_request] or [method inspect_request].

Optional per-phoneme silence override map. Each key must resolve to exactly one phoneme token, and each value is the silence duration in seconds that should be applied when that phoneme is encountered.

Execution provider for ONNX Runtime inference. Set this before calling [method initialize]. [b]CPU[/b] (0): Default CPU execution. Works on all platforms. [b]CoreML[/b] (1): Apple Neural Engine / GPU acceleration. Available on macOS and iOS. [b]DirectML[/b] (2): DirectX GPU acceleration. Available on Windows when built with DirectML-enabled ONNX Runtime. [b]NNAPI[/b] (3): Android Neural Networks API acceleration. Available on Android. [b]Auto[/b] (4): Automatically selects the best available execution provider for the current platform (macOS/iOS → CoreML, Windows → DirectML if available, Android → NNAPI, Linux → CPU). [b]CUDA[/b] (5): CUDA GPU acceleration. Available when the addon is built and run with a CUDA-enabled ONNX Runtime package. If the selected GPU execution provider is unavailable or fails, the engine automatically falls back to CPU.
Optional GPU device index used by [constant EP_CUDA] and, when supported by the runtime package, [constant EP_DIRECTML]. Negative values are clamped to [code]0[/code].

signal.name: initialized
signal.description: Emitted when [method initialize] completes. [param success] is [code]true[/code] if initialization succeeded, or [code]false[/code] if it failed.

signal.name: synthesis_completed
signal.description: Emitted when [method synthesize_async] or [method synthesize_async_request] finishes successfully. [param audio] contains the synthesized speech as an [AudioStreamWAV] (22050 Hz, 16-bit PCM). This signal is emitted on the main thread and is safe to use for updating the scene.

signal.name: synthesis_failed
signal.description: Emitted when [method synthesize_async], [method synthesize_async_request], [method synthesize_streaming], or [method synthesize_streaming_request] fails. [param error] contains a human-readable error message describing the cause of the failure.

signal.name: runtime_state_changed
signal.description: Emitted when the internal runtime state changes. [param state] matches the values returned by [method get_runtime_state].

signal.name: synthesis_failed_detailed
signal.description: Emitted alongside [signal synthesis_failed] with the same machine-readable payload shape returned by [method get_last_error].

signal.name: streaming_ended
signal.description: Emitted when [method synthesize_streaming] or [method synthesize_streaming_request] has finished pushing all audio chunks to the playback buffer. This signal is emitted on the main thread.

constants.constant: CPU execution provider. Default, works on all platforms.
CoreML execution provider for macOS and iOS. Uses Apple Neural Engine and GPU when available.
DirectML execution provider for Windows. Uses DirectX GPU acceleration.
NNAPI execution provider for Android. Uses Android Neural Networks API.
Automatically selects the best available execution provider for the current platform.
CUDA execution provider. Requires a CUDA-enabled ONNX Runtime package and compatible NVIDIA drivers.
